title: The One with All the Rugby
weakest_section: 13:00-13:30 range with mostly physical comedy reactions

jokes.text: You said there were gonna be other guys. There are no other guys. - Chandler, there's a guy right over there. - That's a mailman.
jokes.setup: Chandler complaining about being the only man at the nail salon
jokes.punchline: Monica points to their own mailman as proof of other men
jokes.explanation: Clean setup-punchline with good misdirection. Monica's desperate attempt to prove her point backfires amusingly.

jokes.text: Chandler, don't worry. This doesn't make you any less of a guy. That does. What am I sitting on?
jokes.setup: Monica tries to reassure Chandler about his masculinity
jokes.punchline: Something at the nail salon immediately undermines her reassurance
jokes.explanation: Good timing with immediate contradiction. Chandler's discomfort is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Heh. I'd hate to think what this woman was scratching when this broke off.
jokes.setup: Finding a broken nail at the salon
jokes.punchline: Chandler's gross-out speculation about its origin
jokes.explanation: Mild gross-out humor that fits Chandler's sarcastic observations.

jokes.text: Joey, let me ask you a question. This has been driving me crazy. - What does this light switch do? - Oh, nothing.
jokes.setup: Monica's obsessive need to know what the switch does
jokes.punchline: Joey's complete indifference to the mystery
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast - Monica's obsessiveness vs Joey's laid-back attitude. Sets up the running gag.

jokes.text: Didn't it drive you crazy to not know what it did? I know what it did. Nothing.
jokes.setup: Monica assumes Joey shares her need to know
jokes.punchline: Joey's literal, untroubled response
jokes.explanation: Joey's simple logic perfectly contrasts Monica's complexity. Great character-driven humor.

jokes.text: They wouldn't have put it there if it didn't do something. - How can you not care? - Like this:
jokes.setup: Monica's logical argument about the switch's purpose
jokes.punchline: Joey demonstrates not caring through facial expression/gesture
jokes.explanation: Brilliant physical comedy moment where Joey literally demonstrates indifference. Perfect economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Do you know what that little silver knob on the toilet does? - Sure. It flushes it. - Okay, good. Now, since you know, when you come over, would you mind actually using it?
jokes.setup: Monica testing Joey's knowledge of bathroom fixtures
jokes.punchline: Reveals Joey doesn't flush the toilet
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - seems like another switch question but reveals a gross personal habit. Strong character work.

jokes.text: [SCREAMS] MONICA: Oh, my God. Wow. - Guess who we ran into today?
jokes.setup: Chandler's normal entrance
jokes.punchline: He screams, apparently startled by something
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy reaction, nothing particularly clever or character-specific.

jokes.text: Yeah, I'm riding the alimony pony.
jokes.setup: Janice explaining her divorce benefits
jokes.punchline: Her characteristic crude metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Janice's inappropriate oversharing and colorful language. Memorable phrasing.

jokes.text: And there it is.
jokes.setup: Janice's typical inappropriate comment
jokes.punchline: Chandler's resigned recognition of her behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler reaction - acknowledging the inevitable awkwardness with weary acceptance.

jokes.text: - Bye. Bye. - Bye. Bye. - Bye. Bye-bye. Bye-bye. Bye. - Bye-bye. Bye-bye. Oh.
jokes.setup: Normal goodbye between Chandler and Janice
jokes.punchline: Escalating chorus of awkward goodbyes from everyone
jokes.explanation: Good escalating awkwardness as everyone tries to end the uncomfortable moment.

jokes.text: What? I thought you were crazy about her. You know all those little annoying things she did before we fell in love? You know, like her voice and her laugh and her personality?
jokes.setup: Monica questioning Chandler's feelings about Janice
jokes.punchline: Chandler listing increasingly fundamental flaws, ending with 'personality'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from surface annoyances to her entire personality. Perfect structure with growing absurdity.

jokes.text: My ass is, like, frozen. Yeah, try sticking it in the freezer for 20 minutes.
jokes.setup: Emily complaining about being cold
jokes.punchline: Chandler's literal interpretation as cooking advice
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler misdirection joke, taking a common expression literally. Good economy.

jokes.text: No, it's really not that bad. I mean, I, for one, feel perfectly safe. [LIAM & DEVON SHOUTING AND LAUGHING] Help! Help! Help!
jokes.setup: Emily reassuring Ross that New York is safe
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradicting evidence of apparent attack
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic timing - safety reassurance immediately followed by apparent danger. Good structure.

jokes.text: That was a good one, huh? I was like, 'Help! Help!'
jokes.setup: Apparent mugging/attack scene
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was just friends playing around
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the misdirection setup, though the resolution is fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Well, actually, the last time you and I saw each other was the next morning. Ha, ha. Oh, Liam. Heh. Oh, Liam.
jokes.setup: Emily trying to recall when she last saw these friends
jokes.punchline: Liam's implication about spending the night together, Emily's awkward laugh
jokes.explanation: Awkward sexual implication with appropriate uncomfortable reactions. Ross's silent response is part of the joke.

jokes.text: So, what, were you guys playing soccer or something? Or should I call it... [IN BRITISH ACCENT] ...'football'? Uh...
jokes.setup: Ross trying to sound worldly about British customs
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt at cultural awareness falls flat
jokes.explanation: Ross's awkward intellectual showing off. The 'Uh...' suggests his attempt didn't land.

jokes.text: Ha, ha. Ross play rugby? I don't think so. Heh. What? What's so funny about that?
jokes.setup: Emily's dismissive laughter at Ross playing rugby
jokes.punchline: Ross's wounded pride and confusion
jokes.explanation: Sets up Ross's masculine insecurity plotline. His defensive response is very in character.

jokes.text: Well, I mean, you're American, to start with. You don't really have rugby here. Heh. Well, we didn't have freedom here until 1776 either, so...
jokes.setup: Emily dismissing Americans' rugby abilities
jokes.punchline: Ross's overwrought patriotic comeback
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross moment - intellectual superiority mixed with wounded masculinity. The freedom comparison is absurdly overblown.

jokes.text: Oh, boy. I just love to sing. Yes, I know that you do. One of the reasons people were complaining... ...was because most of the people had paid to hear the actor sing 'Old Man River.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe expressing her love of singing
jokes.punchline: Chandler revealing she disrupted a professional performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - oblivious to social norms. The specific song reference adds authenticity to the absurdity.

jokes.text: Oh, look at us. Who would've thought that Cupid had a station at 14th Street Nails?
jokes.setup: Romantic reconnection at the nail salon
jokes.punchline: Janice's characteristically cheesy romantic metaphor
jokes.explanation: Typical Janice over-the-top romantic language. The specific location makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Right. But I just think this is happening too soon. Oh, too soon, too schmoon. Face it, honey, I am not letting you get away this time.
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to slow things down
jokes.punchline: Janice's dismissive rhyming and determination
jokes.explanation: Janice's characteristic dismissive wordplay and aggressive pursuit. 'Too schmoon' is memorably ridiculous.

jokes.text: Okay, could you just stop talking for a second? Yemen.
jokes.setup: Janice excitedly guessing romantic European destinations
jokes.punchline: Chandler's desperate interruption and completely unromantic location
jokes.explanation: Brilliant comedic structure - builds romantic expectations then completely deflates them. 'Yemen' is the perfect anti-romantic destination. Near-perfect execution.

jokes.text: But I do know it's sometime tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Chandler claiming uncertainty about departure timing
jokes.punchline: Making the timeline impossibly urgent to escape faster
jokes.explanation: Shows Chandler's desperation escalating - making the lie even more elaborate and urgent.

jokes.text: I bet I stopped listening before you did. You know, you also could have used lamps... ...and then followed the light. Yeah, well, I'm using noise. Okay?
jokes.setup: Monica explaining her complex noise-based switch detection method
jokes.punchline: Rachel's obvious simpler solution and Monica's stubborn rejection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica character moment - so invested in her method she rejects an obviously better solution. Rachel's opening line acknowledges the audience's reaction.

jokes.text: It's coming from Joey. Oh, my God. That's so freaky. Turn him off.
jokes.setup: Monica tracking down the mysterious noise source
jokes.punchline: The sound is coming from Joey himself, treated like an appliance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist payoff to the switch mystery. Treating Joey like a machine to be 'turned off' is wonderfully ridiculous.

jokes.text: Dude, you're not even man enough to order the channel that carries the sport.
jokes.setup: Ross claiming he's man enough to play rugby
jokes.punchline: Joey pointing out Ross doesn't even pay for sports channels
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the masculinity theme - from physical prowess to basic cable packages. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: And Yemen. That actually sounds like a real country.
jokes.setup: Learning about Chandler's fake move to Yemen
jokes.punchline: Ross's impressed reaction to the lie's plausibility
jokes.explanation: Ross's academic appreciation for the geographical accuracy of Chandler's lie. Character-appropriate reaction.

jokes.text: - Joey, trade lives with me. - Nope.
jokes.setup: Chandler's desperate plea to escape his situation
jokes.punchline: Joey's immediate, casual rejection
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - Joey's instant, casual 'Nope' to a desperate request. Shows he understands Chandler's situation completely.

jokes.text: Oh, please, Ross. You got hurt playing badminton with my dad. That's because your mom's dog kept looking at me.
jokes.setup: Monica mocking Ross's athletic prowess
jokes.punchline: Ross's absurd excuse about being intimidated by a dog
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Ross excuse. The specificity (badminton, mom's dog looking at him) makes it wonderfully ridiculous and quotable.

jokes.text: Okay, Ross, look, look, look. Right here, it's called a 'scrum,' okay? - It's kind of like a huddle. - And is a hum kind of like a scruddle?
jokes.setup: Joey explaining rugby terminology
jokes.punchline: Ross making up nonsense words in response
jokes.explanation: Ross's intellectual wordplay backfiring. 'Scruddle' is a memorable made-up word.

jokes.text: Well, you should've seen the guy that she used to go out with. I mean, he's like Joe Rugby. You're kidding. And he plays rugby? That's so funny.
jokes.setup: Ross describing Emily's athletic ex-boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's literal interpretation of 'Joe Rugby' as an actual name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - missing obvious metaphors and finding coincidences hilarious. Very character-appropriate.

jokes.text: All right, come on. Look, Ross can take care of himself. It's not like he's Chandler. CHANDLER: Thanks.
jokes.setup: Joey defending Ross's toughness
jokes.punchline: Casually insulting Chandler in the process
jokes.explanation: Good casual insult with Chandler's perfect deadpan reaction. Shows the group dynamics well.

jokes.text: I'll be that guy, right outside the circle.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to sound tough about rugby strategy
jokes.punchline: Admitting he'll avoid all actual contact
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross - trying to sound strategic while revealing cowardice. The visual of being 'outside the circle' is clear and funny.

jokes.text: Oh. Well, maybe there was a dog looking at him.
jokes.setup: Watching someone get injured on TV
jokes.punchline: Monica referencing Ross's earlier absurd excuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback delivery. Monica applying Ross's ridiculous logic to explain someone else's injury.

jokes.text: They don't look any bigger than me. That's because you're closer to you. So you look bigger to you from where you are.
jokes.setup: Ross claiming the rugby players aren't intimidating
jokes.punchline: Joey's convoluted but logical explanation of perspective
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic from Joey. Simultaneously makes perfect sense and is completely ridiculous. Very quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: Okay, I know what I've gotta do. I've gotta go Red Ross. You know, Red Ross? Totally don't know what you're talking about.
jokes.setup: Ross invoking what he thinks is a known alter ego
jokes.punchline: Nobody recognizes his supposed 'Red Ross' persona
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross delusion - thinking he has a tough reputation when he doesn't. Sets up the 'Red Ross' running gag.

jokes.text: That time that we were all waiting in line for Dances With Wolves... ...and that one guy cut in front of us, and I just lost it? Huh? Screamed at him, turned all red. Red Ross! No.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining his 'Red Ross' origin story
jokes.punchline: Joey still has no memory of this supposedly memorable incident
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work - Ross thinking a movie line incident made him legendary. The specific movie reference and Joey's flat 'No' are perfect.

jokes.text: [ROSS SCREAMING]
jokes.setup: Ross entering his first rugby scrum
jokes.punchline: Immediate screaming upon contact
jokes.explanation: Predictable physical comedy payoff. The scream is character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Joey!
jokes.setup: Ross being overwhelmed in the rugby game
jokes.punchline: Calling for Joey like a child calling for help
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's helplessness, calling for Joey like a security blanket.

jokes.text: He asked you out? - No. But I was showing him some cuff links, and I felt his pulse. Saucy.
jokes.setup: Monica assuming Rachel got asked out
jokes.punchline: Rachel's pathetically small romantic progress and Chandler's sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke - from exciting news to mundane reality. Chandler's 'Saucy' perfectly deflates Rachel's excitement.

jokes.text: All I had to do was pay $25 and wait in line for three hours. Wow. If only more people knew.
jokes.setup: Monica proudly describing her bureaucratic achievement
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic response about the 'easy' process
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler sarcasm highlighting the absurdity of Monica's obsessive effort. Good economy and timing.

jokes.text: Hi. I need a fake ticket to Yemen. - One ticket to Yemen? - Oh, no, no, no. I just need a pretend ticket.
jokes.setup: Chandler's desperate attempt to maintain his lie
jokes.punchline: The agent's confusion and Chandler's increasingly absurd explanation
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the Yemen lie. Chandler's desperation leads to increasingly ridiculous requests.

jokes.text: What would you give to a kid if he wanted a ticket to play with? Are you traveling with a child? No.
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to explain his fake ticket request
jokes.punchline: Agent's logical question completely derailing his explanation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy as Chandler's lie gets more complicated. The agent's practical questions highlight the absurdity.

jokes.text: Uh, sir, a ticket to Yemen is $2100, and we don't take library cards.
jokes.setup: Chandler's attempt to use a library card as payment
jokes.punchline: Agent's matter-of-fact rejection of this ridiculous payment method
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man delivery. The agent's professional tone makes Chandler's desperation funnier.

jokes.text: No offense, but sometimes it's hard to understand you... ...you know, with the accent, so...
jokes.setup: Liam claiming he told the team to go easy on Ross
jokes.punchline: Emily suggesting his accent prevented clear communication
jokes.explanation: Awkward cultural moment with Emily being diplomatically rude. The trailing 'so...' adds to the discomfort.

jokes.text: Did you see me? I was pretty good, huh? That is one fun game. - Right. - Do me a favor? Grab me a bottle of water? - Yeah. - Thanks. - I think I'm dying. I really do.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to seem casual and tough after halftime
jokes.punchline: Immediately admitting he's dying once Emily leaves
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy showing Ross's false bravado immediately cracking. The contrast is beautifully executed.

jokes.text: Tell my son that I love him.
jokes.setup: Ross claiming he's dying from rugby injuries
jokes.punchline: Dramatic deathbed confession moment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Ross's dramatics. The specific reference to Ben adds authenticity to his theatrical suffering.

jokes.text: What? No. No, I'm not stopping. I'm Red Ross. Dude, you go back out there, you're gonna be Dead Ross.
jokes.setup: Ross insisting he won't quit, invoking his tough alter ego
jokes.punchline: Joey's escalation to 'Dead Ross'
jokes.explanation: Great wordplay escalation from 'Red Ross' to 'Dead Ross.' Perfect economy and builds on the established running gag.

jokes.text: God, no. That is no place for a woman. Those guys will grab anything.
jokes.setup: Emily offering to help with rugby strategy
jokes.punchline: Ross's sexist assumption about rugby players and inappropriate touching
jokes.explanation: Ross's misguided chivalry creates awkwardness. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Real...? I could use that. Trick hip, no cup. Okay, okay.
jokes.setup: Emily listing opponents' weaknesses
jokes.punchline: Ross's increasingly unethical enthusiasm for exploiting vulnerabilities
jokes.explanation: Shows Ross's competitive side overriding his ethics. The specific injuries make it funnier.

jokes.text: But Liam's on my team. - I don't care. You just get him. - Go get him.
jokes.setup: Emily advising Ross to target Liam's weak knees
jokes.punchline: Emily doesn't care about team loyalty, Phoebe encourages violence
jokes.explanation: Dark turn as Emily and Phoebe encourage Ross to attack his own teammate. Good escalation of moral compromise.

jokes.text: What are these? Oh, just some pictures I made and hung up. I thought they'd brighten up the place. They do, don't you think?
jokes.setup: Rachel questioning strange wall decorations
jokes.punchline: Joey's proud explanation of his homemade 'art'
jokes.explanation: Typical Joey moment - proud of something childish/crude. The visual element we can't see is part of the joke.

jokes.text: I know that there's no hole there. I just really like that picture.
jokes.setup: Rachel startled by what she thought was a hole
jokes.punchline: Joey's innocent explanation about liking his 'art'
jokes.explanation: Joey's childlike pride in his questionable decorating choices. Implies the 'picture' is inappropriate.

jokes.text: [GASPS] But it didn't. - Say hello to Mrs. Catrokis. RACHEL: Oh, my God. WOMAN: Hello, darling.
jokes.setup: Monica following the wire to find what the switch controls
jokes.punchline: The switch controls the neighbor's TV/apartment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff to the switch mystery - it controls someone else's apartment. Perfectly absurd and unexpected resolution.

jokes.text: Look, your eye's still popping out a little. I'm gonna go get some ice. - Okay. - Ooh, ice, yeah. I'm so in the mood for ice.
jokes.setup: Emily noting Ross's injury needs ice
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's enthusiastic agreement about ice as if it's a treat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - treating medical ice like a beverage choice. Very character-appropriate non sequitur.

jokes.text: I made a man twice my size cry. I mean, I haven't done that since I was 4 and I washed my dad's Porsche with rocks.
jokes.setup: Ross boasting about making a big man cry
jokes.punchline: His last similar 'achievement' was accidentally destroying his dad's car as a child
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ross moment combining pride with accidental revelation of childhood destruction. Perfect escalation and highly quotable. The specificity (Porsche, rocks, age 4) makes it memorable.

jokes.text: You know what? You know what? It's not.
jokes.setup: Ross claiming the pain of Emily touching his injury is 'worth it'
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal when she actually hurts him
jokes.explanation: Good quick reversal showing Ross's bravado immediately cracking under actual pain.

jokes.text: Well, just until we find an energy source to replace fuel.
jokes.setup: Janice asking how long Chandler will be in Yemen
jokes.punchline: Chandler's increasingly absurd justification for indefinite absence
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of the Yemen lie into pseudo-scientific territory. Shows Chandler's desperation reaching absurd heights.

jokes.text: 15 Yemen Road, Yemen.
jokes.setup: Janice planning to write to Chandler
jokes.punchline: Her hilariously simplistic addressing system
jokes.explanation: Perfect Janice moment - overly simple and completely wrong address system. Highly quotable and perfectly captures her character.

jokes.text: Well, then I guess I'm going to Yemen. I'm going to Yemen. When we get to Yemen, can I stay with you?
jokes.setup: Chandler realizing his lie has backfired completely
jokes.punchline: Janice planning to follow him to his fake destination
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation payoff - Chandler's elaborate lie completely backfires. His resignation and Janice's determination create perfect comedic tension.

jokes.text: See? I'm doing it. I am totally doing it. I lost it.
jokes.setup: Monica finally operating the mysterious switch
jokes.punchline: Immediate loss of whatever she was controlling
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica moment - brief triumph immediately followed by loss of control. Good final beat to the switch storyline.

tmdb_overview: Chandler encounters Janice. Ross learns rugby to impress Emily. Monica obsesses over an electric switch in her apartment.
